title: celtis ehrenbergiana
intro: Celtis ehrenbergiana, also known as the Desert Hackberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Middle East and parts of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Celtis ehrenbergiana include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and protecting against oxidative stress. The plant is typically prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts for its medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential side effects.
